title: contingency-dsl Composed — Conditioned Emotional Response (CER)
description: Composed procedure schema for CER / conditioned suppression (Estes & Skinner, 1941; Annau & Kamin, 1961). An operant appetitive baseline schedule is maintained while a Pavlovian forward-delay CS-US pairing is superimposed within the same phase. The primary dependent measure is the suppression ratio SR = B / (A + B), where A is the pre-CS response count and B is the response count during CS. Composes schema/operant/ast.schema.json (baseline) with schema/respondent/ast.schema.json (Pavlovian overlay).

ConditionedSuppressionProcedure.description: A single-phase composition: operant baseline concurrent with respondent CS-US pairing.

baseline.description: Operant appetitive baseline schedule. Typically a variable-interval (VI) schedule chosen to produce stable pre-CS response rates. Refers to schema/operant/ast.schema.json#/$defs/ScheduleExpr. Any operant ScheduleExpr is admitted; the linter SHOULD emit a warning if the baseline is not an interval schedule (CER's measurement assumptions presuppose non-zero baseline response rate during pre-CS intervals).

cs.description: CS stimulus reference (identifier or string label). Matches the cs parameter of the embedded Pair.ForwardDelay primitive and any accompanying @cs annotation.

us.description: US stimulus reference. For classical CER, typically an unsignaled brief shock; for appetitive variants, a food delivery. The CS-US pairing is Pavlovian — US delivery is response-independent.

pairing.description: Respondent overlay. By construction a forward-delay pairing (Pair.ForwardDelay), but the schema accepts any respondent RespondentExpr to allow Pavlovian variants (trace, simultaneous) that preserve the composed structure.

suppression_ratio.description: Optional measurement hook. Declares the suppression-ratio computation per Annau & Kamin (1961). Execution layers consume this to configure the measurement pipeline; the DSL itself does not compute the ratio.

pre_cs_window.description: Pre-CS interval duration (A). Typically equals cs_duration for symmetric comparison.

cs_window.description: CS interval duration (B). Typically equals the Pair primitive's cs_duration.
